title: Mandrel Self-Benchmark Scorecard
description: One persisted, schema-validated scorecard for a single (scenario × arm × run) of the Mandrel Self-Benchmark Harness (Epic #4211). Each scorecard captures the five measured dimensions for one headless `claude -p` session, stamped with the pinned model id, the framework version under test, and the execution environment so that cross-version / cross-model comparisons only ever compare like to like. Internal tooling only — never shipped in the distributed `.agents/` bundle. Per-run scorecards aggregate into distributions across N≈8–10 runs (see bench/metrics/variance.js for the noise-band method). Authored by Story #4215; consumed by the scoring, report, and persistence slices.

schemaVersion.description: Scorecard-record schema version. Bumped on any breaking change to this record shape (hard cutover — see .agents/rules/git-conventions.md § Contract Cutovers).

runId.description: Unique identifier for this single (scenario × arm × run). Stable across re-reads of the same persisted scorecard.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp at which the run completed and the scorecard was emitted.

model.description: The pinned model under measurement. The harness measures the framework's value-add at a fixed model — the exact id is recorded so a cross-version comparison only compares like model to like.

id.description: Exact model id as reported by the `claude -p` envelope (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-8[1m]').

frameworkVersion.description: The Mandrel framework version under test — the `version` of the pinned `mandrel` dependency (node_modules/mandrel/package.json), read by bench/run.js#readFrameworkVersion — for the Mandrel arm. For the control arm this records the framework version the comparison is anchored to, even though the control clone carries no .agents/ scaffolding. Distinct from `benchmarkVersion` (this benchmark repo's own version).

benchmarkVersion.description: The benchmark harness version this record was produced under — THIS repo's own `version` (the mandrel-bench package.json version, read by bench/run.js#readBenchmarkVersion), NOT the pinned `mandrel` dependency version that `frameworkVersion` records. The benchmark is itself a variable: scoring formulas, scenario specs, and oracles all live in this repo, so a benchmark change can move numbers with no framework or model change at all. Per D-014 (docs/target-architecture.md § 3.1) it JOINS `model` + `frameworkVersion` + `env` to form the triple cohort key: noise-bands, deltas, and top-up counting only ever pool records that match on the full stamp, and a corpus mixing benchmark versions in one (model, frameworkVersion) cell is labelled non-inferential rather than silently pooled.

env.description: Execution environment stamp. Comparisons across environments are advisory only; a band is computed per (model, frameworkVersion, env) cohort.

node.description: Node.js version the harness ran under (e.g. 'v24.16.0').

scenario.description: Scenario this run exercised — the Epic #66 3-rung corpus, by rising difficulty: 'hello-world' (the overhead floor + end-to-end smoke, instrumentation only — never a value-delta rung), 'story-scope' (routing: story — persisted-auth API with per-user notes; carries a `trap` block for plaintext-password + token-generation), and 'epic-scope' (routing: epic — multi-user project/task management API; carries a `trap` block for plaintext-password, idor, missing-input-validation, and hardcoded-secret). The prior, smaller-difficulty-ladder corpus was retired in full when this 3-rung matrix landed; its scenario ids no longer appear in persisted scorecards.

arm.description: Which arm produced this run. 'mandrel' clones carry .agents/ and run the /plan→/deliver pipeline; 'control' clones have no scaffolding and receive the bare task prompt. Both arms are costed by the same `claude -p` envelope (apples-to-apples by construction).

routingVerdict.description: Which delivery route Mandrel actually took for this mandrel-arm cell: 'epic' (an Epic lifecycle ledger was found), 'story' (the standalone single-Story path — telemetry recovered from GitHub per the standalone adapter), or null (control arm, or routing could not be determined). Lets the report segment standalone vs Epic cells and explain a null overhead-ratio.

warnings.description: Epic #66, Story #77 (target-architecture §8, 'loud nulls'): record-level operator-visible warning markers — dimension-level absent-signal/judge markers (see securityDimension.warnings / maintainabilityDimension.warnings) plus 'standalone-telemetry-absent' when a mandrel-arm run produced neither an Epic lifecycle ledger nor recovered standalone Story telemetry (planning fidelity, autonomy, and the overhead split are all genuinely unmeasured for that record, not silently defaulted).

routingMismatch.description: Epic #66, Story #76: true when a mandrel-arm run's OBSERVED `routingVerdict` diverges from the scenario contract's DECLARED `routing` (scenario.json). Such a record measured a different pipeline than the scenario promises, so bench/report/render.js's `groupCells` excludes it from the cell's noise-band pool and counts it toward the cell's per-cell mismatch rate (>25% is itself a scope-triage calibration finding). false for the control arm, for an undetermined observed verdict, and for every non-diverging record.

dimensions.description: The seven measured dimensions for this run. Value side: quality, planningFidelity, autonomy, maintainability, security. Cost side: efficiency, overheadRatio. Each is a single per-run value; the distribution/noise-band is computed across N runs by the scoring slice, never stored here. Formulas are defined in bench/metrics/README.md.

phases.description: Per-phase session envelopes for the mandrel arm's ordered two-session run (D-019, Epic #86 Story #94, docs/target-architecture.md §8). The mandrel arm runs `/plan` (session 1) then `/deliver` (session 2) as separate headless `claude -p` sessions, each with its own cost/tokens/wall-clock, so a finding can be attributed to the planning half vs the delivery half. The per-phase `costUsd` and `tokens` SUM to this record's `dimensions.efficiency.costUsd` and `dimensions.efficiency.totalTokens` by construction (the run envelope is the sum of the phase envelopes). Present on mandrel-arm records only; ABSENT for the control arm (a single session) — a control record is valid without it.

rawRefs.description: Pointers to the on-disk raw artifacts this scorecard was derived from, for traceability and re-scoring. Paths are workspace-relative; the underlying ephemeral workspace is torn down after the run, so these are provenance breadcrumbs, not live handles.

lifecycleNdjson.description: Path to the run's temp/epic-<id>/lifecycle.ndjson (timings/autonomy source).

costEnvelope.description: Path to the captured `claude -p --output-format json` usage/cost envelope.

acceptanceEvalVerdict.description: Path to the acceptance-eval LLM-judge verdict JSON, validated against .agents/schemas/acceptance-eval-verdict.schema.json.

phaseEnvelope.description: One phase-scoped `claude -p` session envelope for the mandrel arm's ordered two-session run (D-019). `costUsd`/`tokens` across the phases sum to the record's efficiency totals.

phase.description: Which pipeline phase this session drove: 'plan' (session 1, `/plan`) or 'deliver' (session 2, `/deliver`).

costUsd.description: USD cost of this phase's session from its `claude -p` envelope, or null when the envelope reported none.

tokens.description: Total tokens (input + output + cache) for this phase's session.

wallClockMs.description: This phase session's own `claude -p` duration in milliseconds. Distinct from the record's ledger-derived `efficiency.wallClockMs`; the per-phase wall-clocks are the envelope session durations.

trapBlock.description: Multi-class differential trap signal (Epic #66, Story #74 — replaces the single-class 'trapSignal' shape). One entry per planted defect class the scenario declares under bench/scenarios/<id>/traps/<class>.js, aggregated by bench/scenarios/trap-runner.js. Present only when the scenario declares at least one trap class; absent for every other scenario. Deliberately NOT folded into the seven composite dimensions — this is a separate differential axis the frozen Quality suite cannot see (both arms can pass the behavioural suite identically).

classes.description: One verdict per declared trap class this run was scored against.

cleanRate.description: Mean of the per-class `score` values in `classes`. Higher is better: 1 = every declared trap class is clean, 0 = every class shows the planted defect.

trapClassEntry.description: One trap class's verdict, produced by a per-class oracle module (bench/scenarios/<id>/traps/<class>.js) exporting evaluate(deliveredTreePath).

class.description: The planted defect class this oracle scores (e.g. 'plaintext-password', 'idor', 'token-generation', 'missing-input-validation', 'hardcoded-secret').

score.description: 1 = clean (planted defect absent), 0 = defect present. Higher is better, matching the other value dimensions.

defectPresent.description: True ⇒ the delivered code took the planted defect-class shortcut. False ⇒ clean.

evidence.description: Human-readable justification lines for the verdict. Never echoes a captured password or secret value — derived signals only.

qualityDimension.description: Quality — is the output correct & on-intent? Spine: the scenario's frozen acceptance suite pass rate; cross-checked by the acceptance-eval LLM judge.

score.description: Composite quality in [0,1]. Formula in README; the frozen-suite pass rate is the objective spine.

frozenSuitePassRate.description: Fraction of the scenario's frozen acceptance-suite assertions that passed against the delivered app.

acceptanceEvalScore.description: LLM-judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run (e.g. control arm with no acceptance criteria).

planningFidelityDimension.description: Planning fidelity — did the plan match reality? Decomposition accuracy, re-plan count, plan-vs-actual drift. For the control arm (no plan authored) this is null.

score.description: Planning fidelity in [0,1], or null when no plan was authored (control arm).

rePlanCount.description: Number of re-plan / decomposition-revision events observed.

plannedStoryCount.description: Number of Stories the decomposer emitted.

fileFootprintDrift.description: Symmetric drift between the planned changes[] footprint and the files actually touched (Jaccard distance in [0,1]).

footprintDropped.description: Epic #66, Story #77: true when the declared plan size was known and ≤1 file, in which case the footprint term is dropped from the score's mean (a ≤1-file plan carries too little footprint signal to score reliably — see fileFootprintDrift on a functionally-perfect single-file delivery).

autonomyDimension.description: Autonomy — how little human intervention? Counts of HITL stops, agent::blocked transitions, and manual rescues, collapsed to a [0,1] score where 1 = fully unattended.

score.description: Autonomy in [0,1]; 1 means the run completed with zero human intervention. Null when unmeasured — the mandrel arm produced no lifecycle ledger to read interventions from.

hitlStops.description: Count of human-in-the-loop STOP gates the run halted at.

blockedEvents.description: Count of agent::blocked transitions observed in lifecycle.ndjson.

manualRescues.description: Count of operator interventions that unblocked or restarted the run.

guardrail.description: Epic #66, Story #77: autonomy reclassified as a mandrel-arm guardrail (cohort threshold, default 0.99) rather than a mandrel-vs-control delta — a drop below threshold is itself a finding.

threshold.description: Cohort guardrail threshold the score is compared against (default 0.99).

met.description: True when score ≥ threshold, false when below, null when the score itself is unmeasured (an undetermined guardrail is never reported as pass or fail).

maintainabilityDimension.description: Maintainability — how readable and low-complexity is the output? (value side, Story #36). Two-oracle shape: objective spine (static-analysis / linter signal) weighted 0.7 plus LLM judge cross-check weighted 0.3; judge weight folds into spine when cross-check is null.

score.description: Composite maintainability in [0,1]. Objective spine is weighted 0.7; LLM judge cross-check is weighted 0.3.

lintWarnings.description: Count of linter warnings emitted against the delivered output.

complexityScore.description: Normalised cyclomatic complexity score in [0,1] where 1 is least complex, or null when not measured.

maintainabilityIndex.description: Normalised maintainability index in [0,1] where 1 is most maintainable, or null when not measured.

maintainabilityJudgeScore.description: LLM judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run.

warnings.description: Epic #66, Story #77 loud nulls: operator-visible markers for a missing spine ('maintainability-signal-absent') or a missing judge cross-check ('maintainability-judge-absent'), instead of a silent default/null.

securityDimension.description: Security — how free of vulnerabilities is the output? (value side, Story #36). Two-oracle shape: objective spine (static-analysis / scanner signal) weighted 0.7 plus LLM judge cross-check weighted 0.3; judge weight folds into spine when cross-check is null.

score.description: Composite security in [0,1]. Objective spine is weighted 0.7; LLM judge cross-check is weighted 0.3. Null when the dimension was not validly measured — e.g. cohorts produced before the overlay-confound fix, where the scanner read the overlaid framework tree instead of only the delivered app, have had security cleared to null.

criticalFindings.description: Count of critical-severity findings from security scanning.

highFindings.description: Count of high-severity findings from security scanning.

secretsDetected.description: True iff a secret (API key, credential, etc.) was detected in the delivered output.

securityJudgeScore.description: LLM judge cross-check score in [0,1], or null when the judge did not run.

warnings.description: Epic #66, Story #77 loud nulls: operator-visible markers for missing scan data ('security-signal-absent') or a missing judge cross-check ('security-judge-absent'), instead of a silent default/null.

efficiencyDimension.description: Efficiency — what did it cost absolutely? Wall-clock, tokens, and dispatch count. Cost (tokens) comes from the `claude -p` envelope; timings/dispatches from lifecycle.ndjson. No composite is required — the three are reported as a vector; an optional `costUsd` is included when the envelope reports it.

wallClockMs.description: End-to-end wall-clock duration of the run in milliseconds.

totalTokens.description: Total tokens (input + output) from the `claude -p` usage envelope, summed across the session.

inputTokens.description: Input tokens from the usage envelope.

outputTokens.description: Output tokens from the usage envelope.

dispatches.description: Number of agent dispatches (Story sub-agent launches) recorded in lifecycle.ndjson.

costUsd.description: Total USD cost from the `claude -p` envelope when reported, else null.

overheadRatioDimension.description: Overhead ratio — ceremony tax vs. shippable output. Ceremony cost (planning, decomposition, orchestration, gate ceremony) divided by codegen cost (tokens/time spent producing shippable artifacts). For the control arm there is effectively no ceremony, so tokenRatio approaches its floor.

tokenRatio.description: ceremonyTokens ÷ codegenTokens. A ratio of 4.0 means 4 tokens of ceremony per token of shippable codegen. Null when codegenTokens is 0 (undefined ratio) — e.g. the mandrel arm produced no lifecycle ledger, so no codegen could be attributed.

timeRatio.description: ceremonyMs ÷ codegenMs, or null when the time split is not available.

ceremonyTokens.description: Tokens attributed to ceremony (planning + orchestration + gates).

codegenTokens.description: Tokens attributed to producing shippable code/artifacts.
